title: Positive Impact of COVID19 Vaccines at the Individual and Population Level
summary: As more people get vaccinated, we will not only see improvements individually, but its impact collectively, at the population level, with decreasing cases, hospitalizations and deaths.
body: The United States has entered a new phase of the COVID19 pandemic where cases are decreasing, and vaccination rates are increasing. Vaccines not only benefit the individual who has gotten vaccinated by decreasing their chances of getting infected, experiencing disease and death but also benefits the population at large by creating more “viral dead ends.” This two-level benefit is further broken down in the table below to display the positive impact of the FDA emergency authorized COVID19 vaccine. As more people get vaccinated, we will not only see improvements individually, but its impact collectively, at the population level, with decreasing cases, hospitalizations and deaths. In the end, we need more people vaccinated to build community immunity. Every vaccination counts! _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article discusses the United States entering a new phase of the COVID-19 pandemic characterized by decreasing cases and increasing vaccination rates. It emphasizes the dual-level benefits of FDA emergency-authorized COVID-19 vaccines. At the individual level, vaccination reduces the chances of infection, severe disease, and death. At the population level, widespread vaccination creates more "viral dead ends," leading to collective benefits such as decreased cases, hospitalizations, and deaths. The core argument is that increasing vaccination coverage is essential for building community immunity, and every vaccination contributes significantly to this public health goal.
result.main_content_zh: 本文探讨了美国进入新冠疫情新阶段，其特征是病例减少和疫苗接种率上升。文章强调了美国食品药品监督管理局紧急授权使用的新冠疫苗带来的双重益处。在个体层面，接种疫苗可降低个人感染、罹患重症和死亡的风险。在群体层面，广泛的疫苗接种创造了更多的“病毒传播终点”，从而带来集体效益，包括病例数、住院人数和死亡人数的下降。核心论点是，提高疫苗接种覆盖率对于建立社区免疫至关重要，每一次接种都对实现这一公共卫生目标具有重大意义。
